text.title: Sunset vs Fold Framework Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Product killed; team morale tanks; turns out it would have profitably folded into product B.
antipattern.root-cause: Decision made on founder gut; no criteria matrix.
antipattern.fix: Score the 5 criteria (revenue, adjacency, migration, morale, concentration) before deciding.

antipattern.symptom: Product B owner blindsided by inherited capability; team rejects integration.
antipattern.root-cause: Fold decided without receiving owner consent.
antipattern.fix: Receiving product owner must ratify the fold in writing before announcement.

antipattern.symptom: Customers stranded; refund waves; reputational damage on Twitter.
antipattern.root-cause: Sunset announced without named migration path or data export.
antipattern.fix: Publish migration path (alternative product, data export, refund) before kill date.

antipattern.symptom: Decision relitigated every quarter; reorg surfaces.
antipattern.root-cause: Decision verbal; no versioned signed record.
antipattern.fix: Versioned decision record signed by founder + ≥1 VP.
